common.tagline: Solo Immigration Counsel With Direct Attorney Access
common.description: Boutique U.S. immigration representation for visitor, student, family, humanitarian, and urgent court-related matters. Clear strategy, transparent fees, and direct attorney communication.

process.title: How We Work
process.heading: A clear legal process, without the volume-firm confusion.
process.subtitle: The practice is built around one attorney owning the file, the strategy, and the communication from first consult through the filing decision.
process.step1Desc: Start with a paid consultation to identify the issue, deadlines, and the cleanest next step.
process.step2Desc: Build the filing strategy, assemble supporting evidence, and prepare the submission with care.
process.step3Desc: Stay prepared after filing with interview preparation, response strategy, and direct follow-up.

services.title: Our Expertise
services.heading: Focused immigration matters, handled with a clear filing plan.
services.subtitle: From straightforward visa filings to urgent court-sensitive issues, the practice centers on preparation, candid risk assessment, and direct communication.

items.title: Visitor Visa (B1/B2)
items.description: Comprehensive preparation for tourist and business travel visas.
items.stat: Interview preparation built around your actual travel purpose

items.title: Student Visa (F-1)
items.description: Guidance for international students pursuing education in the US.
items.stat: Support from document review through consular interview prep

items.title: Marriage-Based
items.description: Green cards for spouses of US citizens and permanent residents.
items.stat: Evidence-first planning for bona fide marriage cases

items.title: Change of Status
items.description: Switching from one non-immigrant status to another, such as B-2 to F-1.
items.stat: Risk analysis before filing a status change request

items.title: Work Permit (I-765)
items.description: Employment authorization documents for eligible applicants.
items.stat: Category review before submission to avoid preventable delays

items.title: Asylum (I-589)
items.description: Protection for people fearing persecution in their home country.
items.stat: Confidential preparation for high-stakes humanitarian matters

items.title: U Visa (Victims)
items.description: Status for victims of certain crimes who assist law enforcement.

items.title: Advance Parole
items.description: Travel authorization (I-131) for pending green card applicants.
items.stat: Travel risk review before leaving the United States

items.title: Expedite Requests
items.description: Accelerating pending cases for humanitarian or financial reasons.
items.stat: Requests built around documented urgency and agency criteria

about.title: About
about.heading: A boutique immigration practice built for direct communication
about.quote: Immigration cases are too important to disappear into a volume practice. You should know who is guiding the strategy, what the next filing is, and where the risks are.
about.description: M&T is framed as a focused solo practice for clients who want thoughtful preparation, straight answers, and direct access to the attorney handling the work.

pricing.title: Transparent Fees
pricing.heading: Clear legal fees for focused representation.
pricing.subtitle: Ranges vary by complexity, urgency, and document load. The goal is to show realistic legal-fee bands before you book.
pricing.note: These are legal service ranges only. Government filing fees, translations, and third-party costs are separate unless specifically stated in writing.
pricing.paymentPlanNote: Payment plans may be available for qualifying matters. Final scope and billing structure are confirmed during consultation.

faq.title: Common Questions
faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions.
faq.subtitle: Clear answers about process, pricing, and what to expect before you commit to the next step.

faqs.question: How do consultations work?
faqs.answer: Consultations are the first step for most matters. They are used to identify the issue, spot deadlines, review available documents, and decide whether the next move is a filing, further evidence gathering, or a more urgent strategy session.

faqs.question: Do you offer virtual consultations?
faqs.answer: Yes. The practice is set up for remote consultations by phone or video, which makes it possible to review many immigration matters without an in-person visit.

faqs.question: What languages do you support?
faqs.answer: English consultations are supported directly, and interpreter-friendly workflows can be arranged when a client is more comfortable in another language.

faqs.question: Are USCIS fees included in your pricing?
faqs.answer: No. Government filing fees are separate from legal fees. Consultations are used to explain the likely legal-fee range, the expected filing fees, and any other costs that may come up for the matter.

faqs.question: How do payment plans work?
faqs.answer: Installment options may be available depending on the scope of work and urgency of the matter. Payment structure is discussed during the consultation so the client understands what is due before work begins and what can be spread out.

faqs.question: Do you offer sliding-scale pricing?
faqs.answer: Fee flexibility depends on scope, urgency, and the kind of matter involved. If affordability is a concern, raise it early so the office can explain what is realistically available.

faqs.question: How fast can you file my application?
faqs.answer: Timelines depend on the case type, how quickly documents are collected, and whether there are urgent deadlines. Straightforward matters can often move quickly once the required evidence is in hand.

faqs.question: Will you prepare me for my interview?
faqs.answer: Yes. Interview preparation is part of many immigration matters, especially where officer questions, credibility, or document organization can affect the result.

faqs.question: How do I track my case status?
faqs.answer: Status updates are typically handled through email and scheduled follow-up. The exact communication cadence depends on the scope of representation, upcoming deadlines, and whether any agency notice requires a faster response.

faqs.question: Do you guarantee results?
faqs.answer: No attorney can guarantee results in immigration matters because the final decision belongs to the government agency or immigration court handling the case. What you should expect is candid advice, disciplined preparation, and honest communication about risk.

faqs.question: What if my case is denied?
faqs.answer: If a case is denied, the first step is to review the decision carefully and determine what options are actually available. Depending on the posture, that may include a refiling strategy, a motion, an appeal, or a different pathway altogether.

faqs.question: Are you licensed to practice immigration law?
faqs.answer: This website is built around an attorney-led immigration practice. Representation details, licensing status, and engagement terms should always be confirmed during the consultation and in the written engagement agreement.
